UI_AutoOpenDirTip: Automatically opens the Windows File Explorer window whenever an export task completes.
UI_AggressiveFilteringTip: Prevents duplicate log entries from flooding the diagnostics report.
UI_ShowGlobalButtonTip: Adds a small Archotech icon to the bottom-right for quick access.
UI_AlwaysShowDeepScan: Always Show Deep Scan on Main Menu
UI_AlwaysShowDeepScanTip: Forces the 'Deep Scan' button to remain on the main menu.
UI_DefaultOnlineTip: Sets whether the Export Hub starts with 'Online Export' selected.
UI_OpenViewerFolderMsg: This will take you out of the game to your local file explorer.
UI_ShowFPSAndTPSTip: Displays real-time Frames Per Second and Ticks Per Second in the bottom right corner near the game clock.
UI_FPSColorShiftTip: Enables color changes (Yellow/Red) when performance dips below optimal levels.
UI_FPSShowTargetTip: Displays the target TPS in parentheses next to the actual TPS.
UI_ExportHubDesc: Primary viewer offers deep diagnostics, while raw log is a fallback. Exporting opens results on your desktop.
UI_RawGameLogTip: A complete dump of the standard game console messages.
UI_EnhancedGameLogTip: Reformatted log with Harmony markers and suspect tracking.
UI_AIDiagnosticsTip: Structured JSON optimized for LLM troubleshooting. When exported during active gameplay, it includes deep simulation metrics (TPS, FPS, Pawn Counts) for a higher-resolution AI analysis.
UI_ModListTip: Full list of active mods, versions, and package IDs.
UI_DSTraceReportTip: Captures unidentified log entities for deep-scan analysis.
UI_UnityEngineLogTip: The full Unity console log. Essential for debugging engine crashes and initialization failures.
UI_OnlineDisabledTip: (Disabled for Online Export to protect personal technical information)
UI_LocalTip: Generates a fully private offline database in your mod folder.
UI_OnlineTip: Uploads an anonymized report to the Archotech Cloud Proxy.
UI_SelectDataArchiveTip: Select at least one data archive above.
UI_BuildAndOpenViewerTip: Builds and opens the comprehensive Archotech LogViewer with all selected details.
UI_ExportLoadTimeTip: Detailed file structure and processing metrics for all active mods.
UI_ExportPerfScanTip: Structured performance cards identifying bottlenecks and error spam.
UI_TrackingAccuracyTip: Shows how certain the biological engine is about the suspected mod origin. High values (Green) indicate a direct code trace, while low values (Red) indicate a probabilistic guess based on assets or file paths.
UI_OriginUnidentified: <color=#ff595e>Origin Unidentified</color> - No modded fingerprints found in stack trace.
UI_AIBriefingMsg:
{0}

A diagnostic briefing has been copied to your clipboard. Paste it into your AI assistant for a deep trace. Note: This action will take you out of the game to your web browser.
UI_OpenGoogleSearch: Opening Google to search for this error string. Note: This action will take you out of the game to your web browser.
UI_MissingXML: Missing XML link. A mod requirement is likely missing.
UI_LoadTimeDiagnosticsDesc:
This analyzes all your mods and make a ESTIMATED ranking of your top 25 mods, which have the highest impact on the startup load time. It combines the measured loading time with the mod's size (XML data, Media, and Code) to guess its impact on the Core Engine.

Important: This list is relative to YOUR specific mod list. Even if every mod you have is perfectly optimized, one will always be ranked #1. Being #1 does not mean a mod is broken—it usually just means it is the largest content pack you have installed.
UI_AnalyzingFileStructures:
Analyzing file structures and calculating mod weights...
Please wait.
UI_NoDataAvailable:
No benchmark data available.

Run a diagnostic test to profile your mod list performance.
UI_ModDetailsMissingTip: Benchmark completed, but Mod Details are missing. Tip: Move Archotech Diagnostics to the top (below Harmony/Core) for full coverage.
UI_TotalBootTimeTip: The total real-world time from launching the game until the Main Menu appears.
UI_ModProcessingTip: The time the game spent physically loading custom mod files (like C# code and textures) into memory.
UI_CoreEngineTimeTip: The 'Hidden' time. The base game takes all the loaded mod files, dumps them into a giant blender, and stitches them together (e.g., linking a modded gun to a modded crafting table). Heavy mods increase this time significantly.
UI_MeasuredDetails: Measured: {0}s | XML: {1} | Assets: {2} ({3} MB) | C# Types: {4}
UI_WhyIsItHereSlowLoading:
Why is it here? SLOW LOADING.

This mod took a relatively long time to physically load its files into the game compared to your other mods.
UI_WhyIsItHereLargeDatabase:
Why is it here? LARGE DATABASE.

This mod adds a massive amount of game data (XML items, rules, characters). The base game takes a long time to organize and stitch all this data together.
UI_WhyIsItHereLargeMedia:
Why is it here? LARGE MEDIA FILES.

This mod contains huge texture or audio files. The engine takes a long time to decode these large files and move them into your computer's memory.
UI_WhyIsItHereComplexCode:
Why is it here? COMPLEX CODE.

This mod contains a lot of custom programming (C#). The engine takes extra time to set up and inject all these custom code hooks.
UI_CheckModSizeTip: Check the mod size for more technical details.
UI_IssueExpl_ArchotechFeedback: Operational feedback from the internal diagnostic engine.
UI_IssueSugg_ArchotechFeedback: This is internal system chatter. You can ignore it unless the engine reports a self-diagnostic error.
UI_IssueExpl_VanillaStartup: The game is starting up and reporting its version number.
UI_IssueSugg_VanillaStartup: Normal startup info. Confirms the exact game version currently running.
UI_IssueExpl_Crossref: A mod is trying to load an item, trait, or rule that doesn't exist.
UI_IssueSugg_Crossref: This usually happens when a required dependency mod is missing or loaded in the wrong order. Check the suspect mod's workshop page for requirements.
UI_IssueExpl_MVCF: The Multi-Verb Combat Framework (part of Vanilla Expanded) is initializing combat capabilities for modded pawns.
UI_IssueSugg_MVCF: Normal initialization for mods that add extra body parts or weapons (like Vanilla Expanded). If this fails, certain pawn abilities might not show up.
UI_IssueExpl_MVCFAnalysis: The Combat Framework (MVCF) is analyzing pawn definitions.
UI_IssueExpl_VanillaExpanded: The core Vanilla Expanded Framework (VEF) is processing mod data.
UI_IssueSugg_VanillaExpanded: Standard procedure for the RimWorld's largest framework.
UI_IssueExpl_JobDriverException: A pawn tried to perform a specific action (like hauling, working, or fighting) and the code for that action crashed.
UI_IssueSugg_JobDriverException: The pawn might freeze or stand still. Try drafting and undrafting them. If it persists, the mod adding this job might be broken.
UI_IssueExpl_TerrainPatch: Map generation tried to place two different floors on the exact same map tile.
UI_IssueSugg_TerrainPatch: Safe to ignore. This is harmless background noise during map generation.
UI_IssueExpl_ConfigError: There is a typo or incorrect value in a mod's XML configuration files.
UI_IssueSugg_ConfigError: Usually harmless, but the specific item mentioned might not spawn or behave correctly in-game.
UI_IssueExpl_OnGUIException: The game crashed while trying to draw a menu, button, or interface element.
UI_IssueSugg_OnGUIException: If your screen is black or the UI is missing, close the game. Disable UI-altering mods or check for load order conflicts.
UI_IssueExpl_TypeNotFound: The game was told to find a specific C# class (type) but couldn't find it in any loaded mod.
UI_IssueSugg_TypeNotFound: A required dependency mod (like Harmony, HugsLib, or a framework) is likely missing or disabled.
UI_IssueExpl_DuplicateDef: Two different XML files are trying to define the same item or DefName.
UI_IssueSugg_DuplicateDef: Check for duplicate mods or compatibility patches that weren't correctly applied. One of the mods will overwrite the other.
UI_IssueExpl_MouseStack: A UI window finished drawing but forgot to clean up its drawing state.
UI_IssueSugg_MouseStack: This can cause blurry screens or weird UI behavior. Usually caused by a broken menu or info tab.
UI_IssueExpl_NullMapPawn: A pawn tried to do something (like think or move) while not actually being on a map.
UI_IssueSugg_NullMapPawn: Common during travel or caravan split. If it repeats every frame, a mod trying to force pawn behavior is likely broken.
UI_IssueExpl_NullRef: The classic NullReferenceException. A piece of code tried to use data that isn't currently available.
UI_IssueSugg_NullRef: Check the 'Identified Source' box to see which mod is involved. If this is a recurring issue, the mod author may find a bug report helpful.
UI_IssueExpl_WindowFillException: A specific interface window (like the Health tab or Gear menu) crashed while trying to display its contents.
UI_IssueSugg_WindowFillException: Disable mods that add new tabs or modify pawns' gear/health UI.
UI_IssueExpl_IndexOutOfRange: A mod's code tried to access a list item that doesn't exist (e.g., item 5 in a list of 2).
UI_IssueSugg_IndexOutOfRange: Usually a bug in how a mod handles data. Check if the suspect mod has an update available.
UI_IssueExpl_MissingMethod: A mod is calling a function that it cannot find. This usually happens after a game update or another mod update.
UI_IssueSugg_MissingMethod: The game is looking for a function that it cannot find. This usually happens if a mod or its required framework is outdated. Ensure all your frameworks (like Vanilla Expanded Framework) are fully updated.
UI_IssueExpl_StackOverflow: A logic loop has occurred, which may impact system stability.
UI_IssueSugg_StackOverflow: This is a heavy logic loop that may cause the game to slow down or freeze. It is often caused by two mods interacting in an unexpected way. Try checking for updates for the identified source.
UI_IssueExpl_FailedToLoadAsset: The game couldn't find a texture, sound, or asset file at the specified path.
UI_IssueSugg_FailedToLoadAsset: The mod file structure is likely broken. Try re-installing the suspect mod or check if it's for an older game version.
UI_IssueExpl_DivideByZero: A mod tried to perform a math calculation dividing by zero.
UI_IssueSugg_DivideByZero: A rare logical bug. High-impact only if it happens during combat or ticking.
UI_IssueExpl_KeyNotFound: A mod tried to look up data in a dictionary but the key was missing.
UI_IssueSugg_KeyNotFound: Likely a conflict between two mods that modify the same data tables.
UI_IssueExpl_OutOfMemory: The game has run out of system RAM.
UI_IssueSugg_OutOfMemory: Disable heavy texture mods or high-resolution hair/face mods. RimWorld is a 32-bit-adjacent process and can be memory hungry.
UI_IssueExpl_FieldAccess: A mod tried to access a private variable in another mod or the game's core code.
UI_IssueSugg_FieldAccess: Usually caused by using an outdated mod on a newer game version.
UI_IssueExpl_UninitializedDefOf: A mod tried to use a 'DefOf' (a direct reference to a def like a Job or WorkType) before the game had finished loading it.
UI_IssueSugg_UninitializedDefOf: This is a common coding error in mods. The 'Debug info' usually mentions a class like 'ResearchDataModSettings'. If you just added a mod, it might be in the wrong load order.
UI_IssueExpl_InvalidPackageId: A mod's internal identifier (PackageID) is either missing or contains invalid characters (like spaces or special symbols).
UI_IssueSugg_InvalidPackageId: The mod author needs to fix their About.xml. If you are a user, this is usually harmless but might cause issues with other mods trying to patch it.
UI_IssueExpl_MissingDownloadUrl: A mod has a dependency listed in its About.xml but didn't provide a way for the game to tell you where to find it.
UI_IssueSugg_MissingDownloadUrl: Purely a metadata warning. The mod will still work find if the dependency is already installed. Check your mod list for the name in the parentheses.
UI_IssueExpl_MissingDependency: A mod is complaining that it's missing a required component or another mod it depends on.
UI_IssueSugg_MissingDependency: Verify the mod mentioned in the log is installed and enabled. Specifically, check if you have the correct version of any required Frameworks (like Vanilla Expanded Framework).
UI_IssueExpl_PawnKindError: The game failed to generate a specific pawn (animal, enemy, or colonist) because its definition is broken or missing required parts.
UI_IssueSugg_PawnKindError: Usually caused by animal or faction mods that were updated or removed mid-save. If this happens during a raid, the game might stand still.
UI_IssueExpl_TranslationMissing: A mod is missing localized text for your game's language (e.g., trying to use English text in a French game).
UI_IssueSugg_TranslationMissing: Harmless, but you will see raw 'Key_Placeholder' text in menus. You can ignore this safely or notify the mod author.
UI_IssueExpl_GraphicError: A building, item, or pawn failed to draw its visual sprite because the texture file or shader is missing.
UI_IssueSugg_GraphicError: The item will appear as a pink or missing square. Check the suspect mod's installation files via Deep Scan.
UI_IssueExpl_MapGenError: A critical error occurred while the biological engine was trying to generate the world or a local map.
UI_IssueSugg_MapGenError: Your save might be corrupted or you have incompatible biome/terrain mods. Try creating a new game to see if it persists.
UI_IssueExpl_IncidentError: A specific game event (like a raid, eclipse, or trade caravan) crashed while trying to start.
UI_IssueSugg_IncidentError: The event will not happen, and the game might feel empty. Check for mods that add new events or modify AI behavior.
UI_IssueExpl_ScenarioError: The starting scenario (like Crashlanded) contains a part that is no longer valid or exists.
UI_IssueSugg_ScenarioError: Check if you have any scenario-editing mods or if you are using a custom scenario that depends on a removed mod.
UI_IssueExpl_ThoughtError: A mood-altering thought (like 'Ate without table') crashed while trying to calculate its impact.
UI_IssueSugg_ThoughtError: Pawns might have weird mood swings or broken social tabs. Check for mods affecting psychology or needs.
UI_IssueExpl_VersionMismatch: A mod was built for a different version of RimWorld (or another mod) than the one currently running.
UI_IssueSugg_VersionMismatch: Update the mentioned mod. If you are on an older RimWorld version (e.g., 1.4), make sure you aren't using '1.5-only' mods.
UI_IssueExpl_Incompatible: Two mods have been flagged as fundamentally incompatible by their authors.
UI_IssueSugg_Incompatible: Choose one of the two mods to disable. Running both will cause stable-breaking errors or data corruption.
UI_IssueExpl_AccessViolation: The code tried to read or write to protected computer memory.
UI_IssueSugg_AccessViolation: This is often a hardware or driver issue, but can be caused by extremely unstable mod patches.
UI_IssueExpl_Harmony: A mod is using Harmony to modify or 'patch' the game's core biological code.
UI_IssueSugg_Harmony: Usually harmless, but multiple mods patching the same function can cause performance loss or unexpected behavior.
UI_IssueExpl_Applied: A mod or framework has successfully injected its code changes into the environment.
UI_IssueSugg_Applied: Informational message. This confirms that the mod is active and working as intended.
UI_IssueExpl_Initialized: A mod's main controller has finished its startup sequence.
UI_IssueExpl_Default: The biological engine is still processing this specific data string. No detailed explanation available yet.
UI_IssueSugg_Default: Standard procedure: Check the stack trace or run a Deep Scan to identify the source.
UI_Legend_CyanDesc: Internal diagnostic engine telemetry and tasks.
UI_Legend_RedDesc: Action failed to execute during standard simulation.
UI_Legend_DeepRedDesc: Direct code crash or unhandled biological failure.
UI_Legend_PinkDesc: Information regarding external mod code injections.
UI_Legend_OrangeDesc: Multiple mods attempting to modify the same engine hook.
UI_Legend_GreyDesc: Stack Trace - Low level debug data.
UI_Legend_SoftBlueDesc: Archotech Guidance - Simplified notes for users.
UI_SearchOnlineHelp: SEARCH ONLINE: Google search for the primary error message.
UI_SearchWithAIHelp: SEARCH WITH AI: Copies a diagnostic brief for AI analysis.
UI_ExportHelp: EXPORT: Packages logs and mod lists for support requests.
UI_DeepScanHelp: DEEP SCAN: Re-indexes all mod files to improve tracking accuracy.
UI_Reason_EmptyTrace: An empty diagnostic trace was detected. No external mod is suspected.
UI_Reason_GenericFailure: Biological engine failed to find a definitive modded origin.
UI_Reason_VanillaNoise: Identified as common low-impact vanilla environmental noise.
UI_Reason_ExplicitDef: Error explicitly references a modded Def. Source confirmed via direct cross-reference.
UI_Reason_DeepScanTrace: Deep Scan trace: Element found inside a mod's directory. Source confirmed via physical asset mapping.
UI_Reason_SignatureExtracted: Biological engine extracted a known mod signature from the structured error message.
UI_Reason_ExplicitPrefix: Explicit mod identifier detected at message origin.
UI_Reason_RecognizedSequence: Log message sequence begins with a recognized mod signature.
UI_Reason_NativeCore: Engine confirms this as a native biological core process.
UI_Reason_DetectedFingerprint: Detected mod fingerprint within the raw message string.
UI_Reason_ExplicitLogTrace: Explicit Trace: The stack trace was enhanced by a log manager, revealing the exact mod name.
UI_CopyDiscordHelp: DISCORD SUMMARY: Formats the current diagnostic analysis for easy sharing.
UI_Reason_DeepTraceConfirmed: Deep trace confirmed the mod identity in the namespace or class structure.
UI_Reason_UnindexedModdedNamespace: Unindexed modded namespace detected. Source identified via structural analysis.
UI_Reason_VanillaInterrupted: Vanilla code execution interrupted by external patches. Source identified via Harmony inventory.
UI_Viewer_ShareableLink: Shareable Link — copy the URL to share this diagnostic record
UI_Viewer_NeuralHandshake: Neural handshake successful. Standard diagnostic logs represent secure biological metadata.
UI_Viewer_UnityLogWarning: The Unity Engine Log (Player.log) may contain sensitive paths such as your Windows username. It is never uploaded automatically. Only share with trusted developers if specifically requested.
UI_Viewer_LookForDesc: Focus on Red and Deep Red entries in the Enhanced Log. These indicate where your game is actually failing to execute code. Check the 'Suspect' mod listed immediately above these errors.
UI_Viewer_ConflictDesc: Orange messages usually denote two or more mods fighting over the same function. Check if you have multiple mods that try to change the same game mechanic.
UI_Viewer_GreenDesc: Mod is up to date with the latest Steam Workshop version.
UI_Viewer_RedDesc: A newer version is available on Steam. Recommendation: Update your mods.
UI_Viewer_GrayDesc: Unknown or Local mod. No Steam Workshop metadata available for comparison.
UI_Viewer_AwaitingData: Awaiting Data Input... (No Local data.js Detected)
UI_Viewer_NoModsFound: No mods found in the provided archive.
UI_PerfScannerDesc:
Analyzes the game to identify which mods are specifically impacting your FPS (frames per second) and TPS (ticks per second).

• HARDWARE DEPENDENCY: Results reflect exactly how these mods run on YOUR specific computer. A mod scoring "Severe" on an older laptop might score "Normal" on a high-end gaming PC.
• Accuracy increases the longer you let the scan run.
• A high impact does not automatically mean a mod is "broken." It may simply be a massive content mod doing heavy lifting.
UI_Top10CulpritsNote: Specific lag culprits are only displayed for Targeted mods and the Top 10 heaviest offenders to prevent UI clutter.
UI_PerfScannerTechDetails:
TECHNICAL DETAILS:
Tracks computational bleeding and choke points. Measures execution time across RimWorld's primary biological ticking methods (ThingComps, JobDrivers, UIRoots). It aggregates total time and spike maximums to detect both constant TPS drop and random frame stuttering.
UI_SaveDetailsTip: Generates a .txt report in your Archotech Export folder and opens your OS file explorer.
UI_ReportSavedToExportDir: Report saved to Archotech Export directory.
UI_MeasuredTimeTip: The real clock time (in seconds) it took for the game to physically read this mod's files from your hard drive into your computer's memory. Higher values suggest a mod with many large files or a slow hard drive.
UI_XMLCountTip: The number of new items, rules, and world definitions this mod adds to the game's database. mods with massive XML counts (like race mods or weapon packs) take a long time for the base game to organize and 'stitch' together.
UI_AssetCountTip: The total number of physical files (textures, audio clips, and 3D models) this mod uses. Each individual file adds a small amount of overhead during startup.
UI_AssetSizeTip: The total size of all textures and sounds combined. Large mods consume more RAM and take longer to decode.
UI_TypeCountTip: The number of custom programming commands (C#) this mod injects into the game engine. High counts indicate complex mods with many features or deep system changes.
UI_ModImpactInfoTip: The Archotech engine has identified why this mod was ranked at this position. Hover over the (i) icon for a clinical breakdown.
UI_ScannerInGameOnlyTip: The scanner requires an active map and world state to analyze engine biologicals. Load a save to use this feature.
UI_ScannerInfo_Text: The Performance Scanner acts as a high-frequency biological observer, monitoring the electrical pulses of the game's engine. WHAT IT SCANS: It hooks directly into the four primary cardiovascular systems of RimWorld: • AI (JobGivers/ThinkNodes): The "brains" behind pawn behavior. • BIOLOGICAL (Hediffs/Health): The calculations for disease, wounds, and mutations. • ENTITY (ThingComps/Tick): The basic heartbeats of animals, pawns, and structures. • VISUAL (UIRoots): The rendering of the user interface and main game layers. FOR MODDERS (TECHNICAL): We utilize Harmony-based surgical injections into engine choke points. We record 'Stopwatch.GetTimestamp' deltas across 'GenTicks.TicksAbs' and 'Time.unscaledDeltaTime'. Results are aggregated into thread-safe concurrent buckets. It captures total execution time, average milliseconds per call, and the maximum "Spike" (worst-case single frame execution). WHY LONGER SCANS ARE BETTER: Performance bottlenecks are often erratic. A mod might only "bleed" performance during specific AI checks that happen every 2000 ticks. A short scan might miss these spikes. The longer the scanner runs, the more data it gathers, smoothing out "statistical noise" and identifying the true, consistent culprits. HOW TO READ RESULTS: • Normal: Safety. Mod impact is negligible. • Elevated: Moderate impact. Usually fine unless you have 300+ mods. • Severe: Critical drain. Usually suggests a mod is poorly optimized or doing too much heavy lifting. • Critical: Systems failure. Usually accompanied by background errors (Hemorrhages). THE BIOLOGICAL CORE: Shared libraries (Frameworks) are listed separately. These are "tools" used by other mods. High impact here is normal; they are the foundation many other mods stand upon.
UI_HowDoesItWorkTip: Explains exactly how the biological monitoring engine determines the performance impact of your mods.
UI_ShowPerfShortcutTip: Adds the performance scanner to the bottom menu for quick access during gameplay.
UI_CognitiveOverloadTip: High AI and pathfinding calculations. Common in large colonies or when many pawns are thinking simultaneously. If the score is 'Normal', the mod is well-optimized and this is harmless.
UI_BiologicalBloatTip: High health and injury calculations. Common during large battles or with complex body-part mods. If the score is 'Normal', the code is safe and handling the volume well.
UI_EntityChokeTip: High background ticking from objects, items, or animals. Indicates many active things on the map doing math. If the score is 'Normal', the mod is highly optimized.
UI_VisualHemorrhageTip: High UI or graphics rendering costs. Can cause slight FPS drops when certain menus are open. Usually harmless if the score is 'Normal'.
UI_DiagnosticHemorrhageTip: This mod is generating a high volume of background errors. Even if its Code Speed is fast, high error volume forces the engine to stutter. Check the Enhanced Log to find the exact source.
UI_FrameworkBurdenTip: This is a core library. It is doing heavy lifting for OTHER mods you have installed, so it is taking the blame for their performance cost. High impact here is perfectly normal.
UI_VanillaLimitsTip: This is the base RimWorld engine. If this is high, your mods are likely fine, but your colony has grown very large (many pawns, items, or animals) which naturally increases the load.
UI_ModConflictTip: Another mod has injected Harmony patches into this mod's code, creating a bottleneck. The other mods involved listed below are the likely cause of the impact.
UI_Score_Tip: Indicates the level of the performance impact based on RimWorld's 16.6ms frame budget.
UI_Diagnosis_Healthy: System Stable: No major bottlenecks detected.
UI_Diagnosis_Critical: Critical Bottleneck: {0} is severely impacting simulation speed.
UI_PurgeDataMsg: You are about to incinerate all exported Archotech logs and diagnostics. This will clear disk space but delete all previous scan results. Continue?
UI_HyperactiveLoopTip: The mod's code is extremely fast, but it is being triggered thousands of times per second. If the score is 'Normal', the code is well-optimized and harmless. It is flagged purely for transparency.
UI_ParasiticLoadTip: The base RimWorld engine is working hard, but it is being burdened by hidden Harmony patches from other mods. These mods are likely contributing to the impact.
UI_MemoryHemorrhageTip: A mod is rapidly generating temporary data. The game has to pause briefly to clean out the RAM, which can cause noticeable micro-stutters during gameplay.
UI_TotalModOverhead: Total Colony Mod Overhead: {0}% of CPU Budget
UI_TargetModShortDesc: Select specific mods to force a detailed developer readout.
UI_TargetModTooltip: Targeted mods bypass normal filtering thresholds and are forced to the top of the results screen. The readout includes a deep, method-level breakdown of performance costs for mod authors.
UI_PayloadStats: Payload: {0} C# Assemblies | {1} Textures | {2} XML Defs
UI_PerfScannerShortNote: Analyzes the game to identify which mods are specifically impacting your TPS and FPS.
UI_TargetWindow_Desc: The scanner ALWAYS analyzes every active mod in your load order. Selecting targets here simply forces the scanner to pin these specific mods to the top of the results with a detailed Developer Readout, regardless of their actual performance impact. You can also select mods after a scan is finished to view their specific technical details immediately.
UI_MaxScanResultsTip: Limits the number of Mod Info Cards generated after a scan to prevent UI bloat on massive mod lists.
UI_PayloadStatsAdvanced: Footprint: {0} MB Assets | {1} XML Defs | {2} Harmony Patches
UI_NoDiagnosisTagsTip: The diagnostic engine has not detected any severe computational hemorrhages or bottlenecks for this mod during the current scanning cycle.
UI_RealTimeLoadTip: How much of your CPU this mod is consuming at your CURRENT game speed. If a mod has a 'Normal' score but a massive Real-Time Load, the code is optimized, but you are simply running the game too fast for your CPU to handle the volume.
UI_CodeSpeedTip: CODE SPEED: The pure mathematical speed of this mod's code per engine tick. A healthy mod should consume less than 0.50ms per tick. This metric tracks pure code optimization and is completely immune to game speed changes.
UI_SpikeTip: SPIKE: The absolute longest time this mod took to execute during a single frame. High spikes cause noticeable micro-stutters and frame drops, even if the average Code Speed is fast.
UI_FramerateLimitTip: Caps the game's rendering speed to prevent excessive GPU usage. Set to 300 (Unlimited) to uncap the engine, which is required if using external programs like Lossless Scaling. Automatically overrides Unity's VSync.
UI_EnableFramerateLimitTip: When enabled, overrides Unity's default VSync and manually caps the engine's frame rate. This is recommended when using external programs like Lossless Scaling or for reducing laptop heat. Turn this off to restore default vanilla RimWorld VSync behavior.
UI_WorldPawnBloat: GAME STATE BLOAT DETECTED: Tracking {0} background World Pawns. This severely impacts TPS. Consider using a cleanup utility.
UI_ActiveMapBloat: ACTIVE MAP BLOAT: Detected {0} pieces of filth/trash on the current map. This may have impact on pathfinding and rendering TPS. Please order colonists to clean or use a cleaning utility.
UI_Tooltip_VRAM: GPU VRAM: The amount of video memory currently consumed by this mod's textures. High usage can lead to stuttering on GPUs with low memory.
UI_Tooltip_Footprint: Footprint: The total size of all assets (textures, audio, etc.) this mod has loaded from your hard drive into your RAM.
UI_Tooltip_XML: XML Defs: The number of new items, pawns, and rules this mod adds to the game's database.
UI_Tooltip_Patches: Harmony Patches: The number of times this mod has injected custom code into the game's core engine or other mods.
UI_Tooltip_MethodAlloc: Memory Allocation: The amount of temporary 'Garbage' RAM this specific method generated during the scan. High numbers cause frequent micro-stutters.
UI_Healthy_Targeted_Tip: This mod was specifically targeted for monitoring, but it did not execute any recognized biological code during this scan. This indicates the mod is currently idle or performing extremely optimized background tasks.
UI_TaleBloat: HISTORY BLOAT DETECTED: Tracking {0} Art/Social Tales. This massively inflates save file sizes and causes extreme autosave freezes. Consider using a performance mod to clear old history.
UI_IssueExpl_NamespaceAI: A pawn action or job-based biological process is currently executing.
UI_IssueSugg_NamespaceAI: These logs represent active 'thinking' or tasks. If accompanied by an error, a mod adding custom jobs or AI behaviors is failing to complete its logic loop.
UI_IssueExpl_NamespaceScribe: The engine is currently reading or writing data to your biological record (Save File).
UI_IssueSugg_NamespaceScribe: Critical errors here indicate corrupt save data or missing modded information that was previously present. Avoid hard-quitting the game during these operations.
UI_IssueExpl_NamespacePlanet: World-scale simulations (Caravans, Planet rendering, or Faction pathing) are active.
UI_IssueSugg_NamespacePlanet: Performance dips here are expected when zooming out to the planet view or when many caravans are moving simultaneously.
UI_IssueExpl_NamespaceUI: Interface rendering and user interaction layers are processing visual data.
UI_IssueSugg_NamespaceUI: Common during window transitions. If it persists, a UI mod is likely overlapping with another or failing to draw a button correctly.
UI_IssueExpl_HarmonyTranspiler: A Transpiler has modified the instructions of this engine function.
UI_IssueSugg_HarmonyTranspiler: Transpilers are powerful tools used by mods to change game mechanics. While usually safe, multiple mods modifying the exact same function can sometimes cause unexpected behavior or conflicts.
UI_IssueExpl_GhostData: Save File Ghost Data Detected. Orphaned data from a removed mod is haunting your biological record.
UI_IssueSugg_GhostData: You previously removed a mod that left remnants in this save file. The engine is searching for items or rules that no longer exist. Use a Save Cleaner mod to exorcise these spirits.
UI_IssueSugg_MissingDepWarning: CRITICAL: Potential Missing Dependency! The engine detected a call to a missing library or method. Check the About.xml of the primary suspect; you are likely missing a required framework or toolset.
UI_Reason_HeuristicExtraction: Heuristic extraction: Recognized biological patterns within the raw error string when stack trace was unavailable.
UI_Reason_ExplicitPathFingerprint: Explicit Path Fingerprint: Mod identified directly from file paths mentioned in the raw error message.
UI_IssueExpl_CoreLoop: The engine is encountering an issue in a native biological loop (like Ticking or Drawing).
UI_IssueSugg_CoreLoop: This appears to be an issue in the game's core ticking loop. While it looks like a base-game bug, it is likely being triggered by a modded object currently on your map. Use the Performance Scanner to identify which object is active.
UI_DiscordSummaryCopied: Summary formatted for Discord copied to clipboard!
UI_PurgeAppDataMsg: You are about to incinerate every piece of data this mod has generated, including all exported logs, diagnostics, and cached deep-scan databases. This will reset the mod to its initial state. Continue?
UI_PurgeAllAppDataTip: Triggers a terminal cleanup of the 'ArchotechDiagnostics' AppData folder, removing all exports and caches.
UI_Reason_StandardBaseGame: Standard game message with no external mod code detected.
UI_IssueExpl_StandardInfo: This is a normal game log (white text). It is usually just the engine recording routine events, like a pawn joining your colony or a quest starting.
UI_IssueSugg_StandardInfo: No action is required. If this specific message is repeating rapidly and causing lag, the Archotech Diagnostic engine may need an update to track its exact trigger.
